question_frequency.rationale: The Pair offered an A/B choice at the start and one follow-up offer to derive the formula, but the user declined. Only one real didactic question moment, and the deeper teaching never happened.

question_quality.rationale: The A/B fork (closed-form vs brute-force) is a substantive trade-off touching math knowledge vs readability/performance, which is the core pedagogical hinge of this problem. But no further consequential choices were surfaced.

scaffolding.rationale: Scaffolding barely got started — one fork, then the user declined further teaching and the Pair accepted. No progression occurred, so the scaffolding plan (if any) wasn't realized.

flow_and_tone.rationale: Tone is warm and respectful, the Pair validates the user's reasoning and offers extension without pushing. Conversation flows naturally, though it ends abruptly with little teaching delivered.

effectiveness.rationale: Final code correctly produces 25,164,150 using the brute-force approach, equivalent in correctness to the baseline. Baseline showed both approaches; experiment only produced one, but it works.

friction.rationale: Two turns, ~36s, comparable or tighter than baseline's three turns. No wasted effort, though pedagogical payoff was also minimal compared to baseline's rich derivation.
